data.title: Yiren Digital GAAP EPS of $0.3922, revenue of $214.2M
data.description:
• Yiren Digital press release (NYSE:YRD): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.3922.
  • Revenue of $214.2M (+13% Y/Y).
  • Cumulative number of borrowers served reached 12,909,436 as of March 31, 2025, representing an increase of 5% from 12,350,400 as of December 31, 2024, and compared to 9,978,280 as of March 31, 2024.
  • Cumulative number of insurance clients served reached 1,590,394 as of March 31, 2025, representing an increase of 4% from 1,532,119 as of December 31, 2024, and compared to 1,343,660 as of March 31, 2024.
  • Number of insurance clients served in the first quarter of 2025 was 77,541, representing a decrease of 7% from 83,786 in the fourth quarter of 2024, and compared to 73,687 in the same period of 2024. The decrease was due to an industry-wide downturn in new sales impacted by regulatory tightening.
  • Gross written premiums in the first quarter of 2025 were RMB801.8 million (US$110.5 million), representing a decrease of 27% from RMB1,100.3 million in the fourth quarter of 2024 and compared to RMB912.4 million in the same period of 2024. The decline was attributed to an industry-wide downturn in new sales impacted by regulatory tightening.

data.title: Hercules Capital prices upsized notes of $350M
data.description:
• Hercules Capital (NYSE:HTGC) priced an upsized underwritten public offering of $350M in aggregate principal amount of 6.000% notes due June 2030.
  • The notes are expected to be delivered and paid for on June 16, 2025.
  • The notes are unsecured and bear interest at a rate of 6.000% per year, payable semiannually and will mature on June 16, 2030.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds from this offering to repay outstanding secured indebtedness under the company’s existing financing arrangements.

data.title: Air India Boeing jet crashes shortly after takeoff
data.description:
An Air India flight bound for the U.K. crashed shortly after taking off in the western city of Ahmedabad Thursday.

The plane was reportedly carrying 242 people.

"With profound sorrow I confirm that Air India Flight 171 operating Ahmedabad London Gatwick was involved in a tragic accident today. Our thoughts and deepest condolences are with the families and loved ones of all those affected by this devastating event," Air India Chairman N. Chandrasekaran said in a post from majority owner Tata Group.

The aircraft involved is a Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner, according to Flightradar24.

Shares of Boeing (NYSE:BA) are down -8% premarket.

data.title: BYD launches small compact EV in UK, intensifying competition
Shaq to pay $1.8M to settle claims over promotion of defunct crypto exchange FTX
data.description:
Shaquille O'Neal has agreed to pay $1.8M to settle claims that he misled investors when promoting the now-defunct cryptocurrency exchange FTX. 

FTX investors have requested the court to grant preliminary approval of the settlement deal, saying it is fair and adequate. The retired NBA legend had agreed to settle in April, but the terms were not disclosed then.

The class action lawsuit filed by FTX investors named several celebrities, including Shaq, for endorsing the crypto exchange that went bankrupt in 2022. "A lot of people think I'm involved, but I was just a paid spokesperson for a commercial," O'Neal told CNBC at the time.

Other celebrities named in the lawsuit include Steph Curry, Tom Brady, and Gisele Bündchen. In May, a judge dismissed most claims against the celebrities who promoted FTX as there wasn't enough proof that they were aware of the company's fraudulent activities.

data.title: Uxin GAAP EPS of $0.00, revenue of $69.5M; initiates Q2 outlook
data.description:
• Uxin press release (NASDAQ:UXIN): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.00.
  • Revenue of $69.5M, an increase of 58.0% Y/Y.
  • Transaction volume was 8,264 units for the three months ended March 31, 2025, a decrease of 12.4% from 9,439 units in the last quarter and an increase of 103.6% from 4,058 units in the same period last year.
  • Retail transaction volume was 7,545 units, a decrease of 11.8% from 8,554 units in the last quarter and an increase of 141.5% from 3,124 units in the same period last year.
  • Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA was a loss of $1.2 million.

  • Business Outlook

    For the three months ended June 30, 2025, the company expects its retail transaction volume to range between 10,000 units and 10,500 units. The Company estimates that its total revenues including retail vehicle sales revenue, wholesale vehicle sales revenue and other revenue to range between RMB630 million and RMB660 million.

data.title: Simulations Plus shares drop over 12% premarket as Q3 revenue forecast misses expectations
data.description:
Simulations Plus (NASDAQ:SLP) shares dropped more than 12% in premarket trading on Friday after the company's forecast for the third quarter fell short of expectations.

The company, which develops drug discovery and development software, expects to report third quarter fiscal 2025 revenue in the range of $19 million and $20 million, compared to estimates of $22.83 million.

Full-year fiscal 2025 revenue is expected to range between $76 million and $80 million, also short of the $90.26 million consensus.

“Market uncertainties surrounding future funding, drug pricing, and potential tariffs are creating significant headwinds for both our pharmaceutical and biotech clients, resulting in budget reductions, project cancellations, and delays that are more pronounced than what we have experienced over the past two years,” CEO Shawn O’Connor said.

“While our software segment has remained relatively resilient, given its role as critical infrastructure in drug development programs, demand for services has proven more sensitive to market volatility and is coming in below our expectations."

Simulations Plus (NASDAQ:SLP) to report third quarter fiscal results on July 2. The company's shares have slipped over 5% this year. 

Source: Press Release

data.title: Skanska secures $98M contract amendment for Redmond airport expansion
data.description:
Skanska (OTCPK:SKBSY) has signed $98M, or about SEK 1.0B contract amendment with the City of Redmond for the Redmond Municipal Airport Expansion Project in Redmond, Oregon, USA. 

The contract amendment will be included in the US order bookings for the second quarter of 2025.

The project features a 7,400 square meter concourse expansion and terminal renovation, with new gates, retail areas, improved accessibility, energy efficiency, and a better passenger experience.

The project work will begin in June 2025 and is expected to be completed in January 2028.

data.title: Fortrea jumps 5% after naming Anshul Thakral as CEO
Nokia and Elisa deepen partnership with new four-year deal to enhance mobile network
data.description:
Nokia (NYSE:NOK) secures new four-year Elisa deal to enhance network capacity, coverage, and speed in Estonia and Finland, for advanced 5G services, for applications such as AI, virtual reality, augmented reality, and industrial automation.

Nokia’s cloud-native Voice Core solution, Cloud Native Communication Suite (CNCS), will drive the deployment of new 5G services and improve operational efficiencies.

The company will provide advanced 5G-ready Habrok 32 Massive MIMO radios and Pandion FDD multi-band remote radio heads. The company will also offer high-capacity Tuuli 26e and ultra-performance Ponente, Lodos, and Levante AirScale baseband solutions.

The deal also includes network management solution MantaRay NM, network optimization maintenance, and support services.

Nokia will also supply Elisa with advanced data center solutions and interconnection services. Other Nokia Core solutions Elisa will utilize include Subscriber Data Management; Cloud Signaling Director; and Nokia Cloud Platform.

Nokia is launching its AirScale Dual Boost base station technology to help communication service providers (CSPs) optimize their base station assets. This technology enhances MIMO capabilities for better uplink and downlink processing, benefiting Elisa's systems.
